Meta.title: Voxa — Dub any video, keep it in sync
Meta.description: Open-source AI video dubbing. Whisper transcription, context-aware LLM translation, four TTS engines, and placement locked to the speaker. MIT-licensed. Runs on your machine.

Hero.title: Dub any video into another language — and keep it in sync.
Hero.subtitle: Whisper transcription, context-aware LLM translation, four TTS engines, and placement locked to the speaker. MIT-licensed. Runs on your machine.

Demo.label: Demo
Demo.title: See it in action
Demo.subtitle: Original audio, then the Voxa dub — locked to the speaker.

Why.label: Why Voxa
Why.title: The dub never falls behind the speaker.
Why.body: Naive tools concatenate clips and drift. Voxa anchors every clip to the source timeline, so the dub stays locked to the speaker — no growing lag, no re-syncing by hand.

Features.label: Features
Features.title: Everything the pipeline needs
Features.subtitle: A complete dubbing toolchain — transcription to final mux — in a single command.

items.title: Anchored placement
items.body: Every clip is pinned to the source timeline. Drift stays at zero.

items.title: Four TTS engines
items.body: Edge, OpenAI, Piper (offline), and XTTS voice cloning.

items.title: Context-aware translation
items.body: Google, Ollama, OpenAI, or Anthropic — translate with full-sentence context.

items.title: Quality gate
items.body: Optional WER scoring re-synthesizes lines that miss the mark.

items.title: Self-hostable
items.body: Point any OpenAI-compatible TTS endpoint with --openai-tts-base-url.

items.title: Resumable
items.body: Checkpoints let you stop and pick up exactly where you left off.

HowItWorks.label: How it works
HowItWorks.title: Seven steps, one command
HowItWorks.subtitle: Voxa runs a deterministic pipeline from raw audio to a synced dub.

steps.title: Extract audio
steps.body: Pull the audio track from the source video with ffmpeg.

steps.title: Denoise
steps.body: Clean background noise so transcription stays accurate.

steps.title: Transcribe
steps.body: Whisper turns speech into timed text.

steps.title: Merge sentences
steps.body: Group fragments into natural, translatable sentences.

steps.title: Translate
steps.body: An LLM translates with full-sentence context.

steps.title: Synthesize
steps.body: The chosen TTS engine speaks each line.

steps.title: Mux
steps.body: Anchor every clip to the timeline and remux the video.

Models.label: Supported models
Models.title: Pick your engine
Models.subtitle: Mix and match transcription, translation, and speech to fit your setup.

Install.label: Installation
Install.title: Up and running in a minute
Install.subtitle: Requires Python 3.10+ and ffmpeg on your PATH.
Install.note: The voxa command comes from pip install .; you can also run python voxa.py directly.

items.q: Does the dub drift out of sync?
items.a: No. Voxa anchors every clip to the source timeline, so placement stays locked to the speaker.
Yes — Whisper locally, Ollama for translation, and Piper for speech. No cloud calls required.

items.q: Can I use my own TTS server?
items.a: Yes. Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works via --openai-tts-base-url.
Whisper covers around 100 source languages; targets depend on the TTS engine you choose.
Yes, Voxa is MIT-licensed. Some optional engines carry their own licenses.

Author.label: Author
Author.title: Built by Servoogle
Author.body: Voxa is an open-source project by Servoogle, created and maintained by Akshin Miranov — practical, self-hostable tools with no lock-in and no telemetry.

Footer.tagline: Dub any video, keep it in sync.
